title: Intensely Yeasty Portuguese Yellow Split Peas Stew
description: A dense and savory Portuguese stew featuring yellow split peas, perfect for midnight cravings, made with beurre-monte and sugar-free friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a copper pot.
instructions: Rinse the yellow split peas and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
In a copper pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the beurre monte, salt, and black pepper, stirring until the beurre monte is fully incorporated.
Add the soaked and drained yellow split peas, chicken broth, and active dry yeast to the copper pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 45 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew to the desired consistency.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
